rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING VS. GRISEZ BUCHANAN, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 1. CROSS DEFENDANTS KEVIN KISH, DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant states that "both counts in the cross-complaint are based on a breach of a mandatory duty pursuant to Government Code 815.6."(Opposition 3:19-8-19). Cross-complainant failed to allege facts supporting a breach of mandatory duty under Government Code 815.6. 2 CCR 10058 contains no such duty ("when the statutorily prescribed act involves debatable issues over whether the steps taken by the entity adequately fulfilled its obligation, we believe the act necessarily embodies discretionary determinations by the agency regarding how best to fulfill the mandate, and this discretion removes the duty from the type of activity that supports a claim under section 815.6." San Mateo Union High School Dist. v. County of San Mateo (2013) 213 Cal. App. 4th 418, 431, citing de Villers v. County of San Diego (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 238) and is not an enactment designed to protect against the risk of a particular kind of injury complained of in the Cross-Complaint. Plaintiff shall have a 10 day leave to amend to allege any cause of action based on actionable breach of a mandatory duty pursuant to Government Code section 815.6. All amendments shall be in bold font. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING VS. GRISEZ BUCHANAN, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 1. CROSS DEFENDANTS KEVIN KISH, DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to punitive damages. All amendments shall be in bold font. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: U.S. BANK, N.A AS TRUSTEE VS. RAUL VASQUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 10, 2018, Line 5. PLAINTIFF U.S. BANK, N.A AS TRUSTEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. Moving party carried its burden. Opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. The only issue before the Court in an unlawful determiner is possession of 1278 Thomas. Determinations of title and compliance with non-judicial foreclosure provisions (except CC 2924 procedural requirements) are not properly before this Court. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA BURTON VS. JOHNNY RAY BURTON ET AL
